name: Pirsch Analytics
what_it_does: Pirsch is a German privacy-first, cookieless web analytics API that tracks pageviews, sessions, and custom events without cookies or fingerprinting, with full GDPR compliance and EU-only data storage.
use_cases: Query pageview counts, bounce rates, and session durations for a given domain and date range to build traffic reports
Retrieve referrer and UTM source breakdowns to power content and SEO agents with attribution data
Fetch custom event counts and metadata to programmatically measure conversion rates for specific interactions
List active visitors in real-time to trigger dynamic content decisions or capacity-scaling logic
Pull country and device breakdowns to segment audiences for localized content generation pipelines
not_for: Individual user tracking or session replay — Pirsch intentionally avoids any personally identifiable data
Complex funnel analytics with multi-step attribution across devices or campaigns
Applications requiring data storage outside the EU — Pirsch stores all data in Germany only
best_when: You operate in a GDPR-sensitive European context and need a simple, accurate, cookieless analytics REST API with German data residency and no consent banner requirement.
avoid_when: You need user-level tracking, A/B testing, session replay, or analytics data stored outside the European Union.

auth.notes: Uses OAuth2 client credentials flow to obtain a bearer token; client ID and secret issued per integration. Access tokens expire after 2 hours and must be refreshed.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pirsch Plus from ~$6/month (100k pageviews); scales by pageview volume with unlimited sites
pricing.notes: Pricing is straightforward pageview-based; no per-seat fees. Annual billing offers discount.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; contact support for high-volume API access

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return structured JSON with error and validation details; error codes are descriptive but not always machine-actionable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All analytics query endpoints are read-only GET requests and fully idempotent; the tracking ingestion endpoint is append-only.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 access tokens expire every 2 hours — agents must implement token refresh using the client credentials flow before expiry
Domain verification is required before data appears; agents provisioning new sites must wait for verification before querying
All data is EU-only; any compliance workflow assuming multi-region data portability will fail
Date parameters must be in YYYY-MM-DD format; ISO 8601 datetime strings are not accepted by all endpoints
The API does not paginate most statistical endpoints — very large date ranges may return oversized payloads that should be chunked by date

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 client credentials with short-lived tokens is a strong auth model; all data stored in Germany under strict GDPR controls; no cookies or fingerprinting reduces breach surface.
